skill.name: security-scanning-security-dependencies
skill.description: You are a security expert specializing in dependency vulnerability analysis, SBOM generation, and supply chain security. Scan project dependencies across ecosystems to identify vulnerabilities, assess risks, and provide automated remediation strategies.
skill.summary: Multi-ecosystem dependency vulnerability scanner with SBOM generation and automated remediation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate defensive security skill for scanning project dependencies across multiple ecosystems (npm, pip, go, cargo). The static analyzer detected patterns typical of security tooling: subprocess calls to run standard vulnerability scanners (npm audit, safety, govulncheck, cargo audit), network requests to public vulnerability databases, and filesystem operations for reading project files. All detected patterns are legitimate security best practices with no malicious intent. The skill helps users identify vulnerable dependencies, generate SBOMs, and create remediation plans.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for Security Tools
medium_findings.description: The skill executes subprocess commands to run standard security scanners (npm audit, safety, govulncheck, cargo audit). These are legitimate defensive security tools with hardcoded arguments and no user input injection vector.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of subprocess calls to known security tools (npm audit, safety, govulncheck, cargo audit). Arguments are hardcoded strings, not user input. No command injection risk exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Multi-Ecosystem Security Scanner
dangerous_patterns.description: Standard dependency vulnerability scanning across npm, pip, go, and cargo ecosystems using established security tools
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is legitimate defensive security tooling - the skill runs known vulnerability scanners (npm audit, safety, govulncheck, cargo audit) to help users identify vulnerable dependencies. No malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Automatically scan project dependencies across npm, pip, Go, and Rust ecosystems to identify security vulnerabilities, generate SBOMs, and create remediation plans.
actual_capabilities: Scan npm dependencies using npm audit for known vulnerabilities
Scan Python dependencies using safety and pip-audit tools
Scan Go dependencies using govulncheck for vulnerabilities
Scan Rust dependencies using cargo audit
Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) in CycloneDX format
Create prioritized remediation plans based on severity and CVSS scores
limitations: Requires dependency manifest files (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml) to be present
Cannot scan dependencies that are not declared in lockfiles or manifest files
Does not perform runtime security testing or dynamic analysis
Network access may be required to query vulnerability databases

use_cases.title: Pre-release Security Audit
use_cases.description: Scan all project dependencies before release to identify and remediate critical vulnerabilities

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate automated dependency scanning into continuous integration workflows to catch vulnerabilities early

use_cases.title: Compliance SBOM Generation
use_cases.description: Generate Software Bill of Materials for compliance requirements and supply chain transparency

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dependency Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan the dependencies in this project for vulnerabilities. Check npm, Python, Go, and Rust dependencies if present.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick security check of all dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Generate SBOM
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in CycloneDX format for this project. Include all dependencies from detected ecosystems.

prompt_templates.title: Critical Vulnerability Report
prompt_templates.prompt: List all critical and high severity vulnerabilities found in the dependencies. For each, show the package name, current version, fixed version, and CVE if available.

prompt_templates.title: Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized remediation plan for all vulnerabilities found. Group by severity and suggest upgrade paths for each affected package.

output: Run 'npm audit fix' to apply available patches

best_practices: Run dependency scans regularly, ideally on every commit via CI/CD
Prioritize fixing critical and high severity vulnerabilities first
Always test your application after updating dependencies
Maintain an up-to-date SBOM for compliance and incident response
anti_patterns: Do not ignore vulnerability warnings without proper risk assessment
Avoid running auto-fix without reviewing changes first
Do not skip scanning due to time pressure - vulnerabilities can be exploited
Never commit updated lockfiles without testing the changes

faq.question: What package ecosystems does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports npm (JavaScript/Node.js), pip (Python), Go modules, and Cargo (Rust) ecosystems.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: Yes, network access is needed to query vulnerability databases like the npm registry, Python Security DB, and Go Vulnerability Database.

faq.question: Can this skill auto-fix vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill can suggest fixes and generate remediation plans, but it recommends manual review before applying any changes.

faq.question: What is an SBOM and why do I need one?
faq.answer: SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) is a list of all components in your software. It is required for compliance (like NIST executive order) and helps respond quickly to newly discovered vulnerabilities.

faq.question: Does this skill work with monorepos?
faq.answer: Yes, the scanner detects dependency files in subdirectories and can scan multiple package managers within a single repository.

faq.question: How are vulnerabilities prioritized?
faq.answer: Vulnerabilities are prioritized by CVSS score, availability of exploits, and whether a fix is available. Critical and high severity issues are flagged for immediate attention.
